block-definition.comment:
Connect to a broker
The options are not required but can be filled if needed
1. username (some brokers need this) 2. password (some brokers need this) 3. keepalive (default time is 60 seconds but some brokers need a shorter time) 4. connection callback (ringed set of scripts to be run when connection is made or remade)
ADVANCED: The brokers offered in the menu are accessed using default ports and basepaths (if needed). If accessing other brokers, the complete urls should be formed like this:
siteURL:port/basepath e.g mqtt.eclipseprojects.io:443/mqtt
It is possible to have more than one connection to the same broker, for instance, using two different user/pass accounts simultaneously. To do this, add a connection id at the end of the broker:
e.g mqtt.eclipseprojects.io:443/mqtt|1

block-definition.s: $cloudOutline MQTT subscribe to %'broker' topic %'topic' $nl on each message run %'callback' %'options'
block-definition.comment:
Subscribe to a topic on a broker
Make sure you have already run an MQTT connect block before using this one
You should add a script to be run inside he grey ring.
It will be run each time a message is received
If you click on the arrow on the grey ring twice, you can use #1 and #2 inside your script.
#1 will contain the received payload
#2 will contain the received topic name
The options are not required but can be filled if needed
1. Subscribed QoS (0,1 or 2) Note this will be downgraded if it exceeds the QoS of the stored message held on the broker
2. Binary mode (boolean) Default (false) - payload is returned as text
If true, payload returned as single column list of values in the range of 0 to 255..

Publish a message to an MQTT broker
The options are not required but can be filled if needed
1. QoS (quality of service 0,1 or 2 - see https://www.emqx.com/en/blog/introduction-to-mqtt-qos)
2. Retain flag (boolean)
3. Binary mode (boolean). Default (false) - the payload is published as text
If set to true then payload is expected to be a single column list of values in the 0 to 255 range and is published to the broker as a buffer of bytes.
Note: If the payload is a list and you don't set the buffer mode option, it will be automatically be converted into JSON and sent as text. Note that many brokers are configured to limit the payload size to 1 MB.

Options:
1. Sub binary mode (boolean) Default (false) - received payload is returned as text.
If true then subscribed, received payload is returned as single column list of values in the range of 0 to 255.. Default (false) - it is returned as text
2. Pub binary mode (boolean). Default (false) - the payload is published as text.
If set to true then published payload is expected to be a single column list of values in the 0 to 255 range and is published to the broker as a buffer of bytes.
Note: If the payload is a list and you don't set the buffer mode option, it will be automatically be converted into JSON and sent as text

input.expand: sub binary mode pub binary mode

custom-block.s: $cloudOutline MQTT subscribe to %s topic %s %br on each message run %cmdRing %group%n%b

block-definition.s: $cloudOutline MQTT request %'broker' topic %'topic' $nl response topic %'rtopic' payload %'payload' $nl on response run %'callback' %'options'
block-definition.comment:
The options are not required but can be filled if needed.
1. Append response topic (boolean): If true, the response topic is added at the end of the call topic after @ sign. The default condition (true) response topic is added.
2. Sub binary mode (boolean) Default (false) - received payload is returned as text.
If true,then the subscribed, received payload is returned as a single column list of values in the range of 0 to 255..
3. Pub binary mode (boolean). Default (false) - the payload is published as text.
If set to true then published payload is expected to be a single column list of values in the 0 to 255 range and is published to the broker as a buffer of bytes.
Note: If the payload is a list and you don't set the buffer mode option, it will be automatically be converted into JSON and sent as text

input.expand: append response topic sub binary mode pub binary mode

custom-block.s: $cloudOutline MQTT subscribe to %s topic %s %br on each message run %cmdRing %group%n%b

block-definition.s: $cloudOutline MQTT example response reporter payload %'payload' topic %'topic'
block-definition.comment: This is a simple echo example. Topic can be used to filter the response.

block-definition.s: $cloudOutline MQTT request %'broker' topic %'topic' $nl response topic %'rtopic' payload %'payload' %'options'
block-definition.comment:
The response topic should be a unique value for each call e.g. use timer
The options are not required but can be filled if needed
1. Timeout value - default is 3 seconds
2. Append response topic (boolean): If true, the response topic is added at the end of the call topic after @ sign. The default condition (true) response topic is added.
3. Sub binary mode (boolean) Default (false) - received payload is returned as text.
If true, subscribed, received payload returned as single column list of values in the range of 0 to 255.. Default (false) - it is returned as text
4. Pub binary mode (boolean). Default (false) - the payload is published as text.
If set to true then published payload is expected to be a single column list of values in the 0 to 255 range and is published to the broker as a buffer of bytes.
Note: If the payload is a list and you don't set the buffer mode option, it will be automatically be converted into JSON and sent as text

input.expand: timeout append response topic sub binary mode pub binary mode

custom-block.s: $cloudOutline MQTT subscribe to %s topic %s %br on each message run %cmdRing %group%n%b

block-definition.comment: Encode costume, sound or data to Base64

Modify variadic defaults to be able to work for blank entries
Any blank defaults should be set as zero width spaces which are removed by this reporter
This is needed for the username and password option in the connect block in the MQTT library
Decode costume, sound or data from Base64

Open (edit) this block to look at examples how to use the MQTT Extension blocks
Background documentation: https://github.com/pixavier/mqtt4snap
Last source code version (mqtt.xml and mqttExtension.js): https://gitlab.com/cymplecy/Snap/-/tree/master/libraries

scripts.comment: Step 0: Connect to the broker
PubSub Hello World!
Step 1: Subscribe to a topic

custom-block.s: $cloudOutline MQTT subscribe to %s topic %s %br on each message run %cmdRing %group%n%b

scripts.comment: Step 2: Publish a message

custom-block.s: $cloudOutline MQTT subscribe to %s topic %s %br on each message run %cmdRing %group%n%b

scripts.comment: Step 1: Activate the responder as a simple subscription

custom-block.s: $cloudOutline MQTT request %s topic %s %br response topic %s payload %mlt %group%n%b%b%b

scripts.comment: Step 2: Send a request and wait the answer.
To execute the examples, single click on blocks, following the corresponding steps.
Step 2 bis: Send a request and do not wait for the answer
Synchronous client-server Hello World! with dynamic response topic (timer)
Step 1: Activate the responder

custom-block.s: $cloudOutline MQTT request %s topic %s %br response topic %s payload %mlt %group%n%b%b%b

scripts.comment: Step 2: Send a request (timer acts as idCall) and wait for the answer
Asynchronous client-server Hello World! with dynamic response topic (timer)

custom-block.s: $cloudOutline MQTT request %s topic %s %br response topic %s payload %mlt %br on response run %cmdRing %group%b%b%b

scripts.comment: Step 2 bis: Send a request (timer acts as idCall) and do not wait for the answer

custom-block.s: $cloudOutline MQTT example response reporter payload %mlt topic %txt

scripts.comment: Asynchronous client-server Hello World! with static response topic (name2)

custom-block.s: $cloudOutline MQTT request %s topic %s %br response topic %s payload %mlt %br on response run %cmdRing %group%b%b%b

scripts.comment: Synchronous client-server Hello World! with static response topic (name2)

custom-block.s: $cloudOutline MQTT subscribe to %s topic %s %br on each message run %cmdRing %group%n%b
$cloudOutline MQTT subscribe to %s topic %s %br on each message run %cmdRing %group%n%b
$cloudOutline MQTT subscribe to %s topic %s %br on each message run %cmdRing %group%n%b

block-definition.comment:
Reports True if the first input string contains the second input string, otherwise false.
Comparison is case-independent by default; use USE CASE-INDEPENDENT COMPARISONS to change that.
block-definition.translations: pt:o texto de _ antes de _ ca:text _ conté _ ? pl:tekst _ zawiera _

block-definition.s: connect data viewer channel %'channel' on click %'onclick'

custom-block.s: $cloudOutline MQTT subscribe to %s topic %s %br on each message run %cmdRing %group%n%b
$cloudOutline MQTT subscribe to %s topic %s %br on each message run %cmdRing %group%n%b

block-definition.comment: Reports the character position (starting from 1) of the beginning of the first input string inside the second input string. If it's not found, reports 0.
block-definition.translations: pt:a posição de _ em _ ca:posició de _ en _ pl:pozycja _ w _

block-definition.s: load gltf %'filename' with id %'name' on load run %'cback'
pose id %'name' to x %'x' y %'y' z %'z' rx %'rx' ry %'ry' rz %'rz'
set texture of id %'name' to image %'img' horizontal %'ht' vertical %'vt' times
show axes of id %'name' with length %'length'
set opacity of id %'name' to value %'opacity'
new cylinder r %'r' length %'length' with id %'name'

custom-block.s: new parallelpipped base %s height %s length %s with id %s

block-definition.s: new parallelpipped base %'base' height %'height' length %'length' with id %'name'
new sphere r %'r' arc %'arc' with id %'name'
new cone r1 %'r1' r2 %'r2' length %'length' arc %'arc' with id %'name'
new torus r %'r' r tube %'rTube' arc %'arc' with id %'name'

custom-block.s: get light 1 placement and set to resp

block-definition.s: get light 1 placement and set to resp

custom-block.s: get light 2 placement and set to resp

block-definition.s: get light 2 placement and set to resp
substract id %'name2' from id %'name1' to id %'name3'
intersect id %'name2' with id %'name1' to id %'name3'
union id %'name2' with id %'name1' to id %'name3'
add JSON to viewer %'json' with title %'title' and id %'idTable'
JSON highlight attrs path %'path' row index path %'rowIndexPath' with id %'idTable'
add chart to viewer %'json' with title %'title' and id %'idTable'

pose id %s to x %s y %s z %s rx %s ry %s rz %s
load gltf %s with id %s on load run %cmdRing
pose id %s to x %s y %s z %s rx %s ry %s rz %s
load gltf %s with id %s on load run %cmdRing
pose id %s to x %s y %s z %s rx %s ry %s rz %s
load gltf %s with id %s on load run %cmdRing
pose id %s to x %s y %s z %s rx %s ry %s rz %s

pose id %s to x %s y %s z %s rx %s ry %s rz %s

pose id %s to x %s y %s z %s rx %s ry %s rz %s

block-definition.s: set async sheet url %'url' name %'sheetname' column %'column' row num %'rownum' value %'value'
set sheet url %'url' name %'sheetname' column %'column' row num %'rownum' value %'value'
set async sheet url %'url' name %'sheetname' cell or range %'cellrange' value %'value'
get sheet url %'url' name %'sheetname' column %'column' row num %'rownum'
get sheet url %'url' name %'sheetname' cell or range %'cellrange'

block-definition.comment: takes a snapshot with the webcam and reports it as a new costume, or zero if the user cancels
block-definition.translations: pt:$camera a imagem actual do vídeo pl:$camera zdjęcie

block.comment: reshape the kernel to match the input matrix's channels, if any
for every padded pixel create a partial copy of the input, item-multiply it with the kernel and take its overall sum (∑)

inputs.input: blurblur sharpen outline emboss edge={ left right top bottom } Gauss
blurblur sharpen outline emboss edge={ left right top bottom } Gauss

block-definition.comment: make a rectangle costume with the given color, width and height. The color can be either specified by the color picker from the input slot, or it can be a list of r-g-b-a values.

block-definition.s: executa escenari %'param' amb valors %'valors' i reset a %'base'

set sheet url %s name %s column %s row num %s value %s
set sheet url %s name %s column %s row num %s value %s
